For language quality, we ask annotators to rate model generation on four qualities and report the averaged score: grammar, fluency, story flow and interestingness of the story. For the prosocial implication, instead of directly asking evaluators to score the level of moral acceptability of the story, we resort to four theoretically moral dimensions from the Moral Foundation Theory (David Dobolyi, 2021) to measure moral implications indirectly: care/harm (“an ability to feel (and dislike) the pain of others, e.g., kindness, gentleness, nurturance”), fairness/cheating: (“the evolutionary process of reciprocal altruism, e.g., justice, rights, autonomy”), loyalty/betrayal (“related to our long history as tribal creatures able to form shifting coalitions, e.g., patriotism, self-sacrifice for the group”), sanctity/degradation (“shaped by the psychology of disgust and contamination, e.g., striving to live in an elevated, less carnal, more noble way.”). In addition to the four theoretically motivated dimensions, we ask evaluators to assess the impacts or consequences to the main and other characters (i.e., if the characters are positively or negatively affected) at the end of the story and how well the beneficiary of morality is attributed as inspired by (Hendrycks et al., 2021b; Lourie et al., 2021b). Each generated story is evaluated by three annotators. Human evaluation templates are shown in Figure 11 and 12 in Appendix §E.
items.zh: 我们根据两个主要标准评估模型生成：语言质量和生成故事的亲社会含义。我们对两个分数都采用人工评估。对于语言质量，我们要求注释者根据四个质量指标对模型生成进行评分，并报告平均分数：语法、流畅性、故事流畅性和故事趣味性。对于亲社会含义，我们不直接要求评估者评分故事的道德可接受性水平，而是借助道德基础理论（David Dobolyi, 2021）中的四个理论道德维度间接衡量道德含义：关爱/伤害（“感受（并厌恶）他人痛苦的能力，例如善良、温柔、养育”）、公平/欺骗（“互惠利他主义的进化过程，例如正义、权利、自主”）、忠诚/背叛（“与我们作为能够形成变动联盟的部落生物的悠久历史相关，例如爱国主义、为群体自我牺牲”）、纯洁/堕落（“由厌恶和污染心理学塑造，例如努力以更高尚、更少肉欲、更崇高的方式生活”）。除了四个理论驱动的维度外，我们要求评估者评估故事结束时对主角和其他角色的影响或后果（即角色是否受到正面或负面影响），以及道德受益者的归属程度，受Hendrycks等人（2021b）和Lourie等人（2021b）启发。每个生成的故事由三名注释者评估。人工评估模板见附录§E中的图11和图12。

items.en: As shown in Table 7, Delphi-enhanced story generation results in the highest prosocial implication scores across all dimensions, beating the strongest baselines for 12.1% to 30.5% relative improvements, without sacrificing language quality. As we hypothesized, our results show that positive sentiments alone do not have as large of an impact on the moral implication of generated stories as influenced by Delphi. Notably, as shown in Table 8, Delphi guides the model to avoid morally questionable content such as “Sandy is Louie’s mother. They have been married for many years,” or “he was happy to make them jealous.” Through the simple experiment setup, we show the power of using Delphi as a plugin sub-module to inform other less principled language generation models to generate contents that are more morally informed and safe.
items.zh: 如表7所示，Delphi增强的故事生成在所有维度上获得了最高的亲社会含义分数，相对改进比最强基线高出12.1%至30.5%，且不牺牲语言质量。正如我们假设的，我们的结果表明，仅凭正面情感对生成故事道德含义的影响不如Delphi那么大。值得注意的是，如表8所示，Delphi引导模型避免道德上有问题的内容，例如“Sandy是Louie的母亲。他们已经结婚多年”或“他很高兴让他们嫉妒”。通过简单的实验设置，我们展示了使用Delphi作为插件子模块来指导其他原则性较弱的语言生成模型生成更符合道德和安全的内容的力量。

full.sec: 6.3 Transferring Knowledge of Delphi to Varied Moral Frameworks
full.secZh: 6.3 将德尔斐知识迁移到不同道德框架

items.en: benchmark (Hendrycks et al., 2021a) offers five challenging tasks designed to assess language models’ knowledge about five prominent moral frameworks: justice, deontology, virtue, utilitarianism and commonsense morality. Details of the ETHICS benchmark are introduced in §3.1. Table 23 in Appendix §F shows examples of tasks from ETHICS. We already include the short scenarios from the commonsense morality task in the original training data of Delphi. Data for the other tasks and long scenarios from the commonsense morality task do not appear in the data to pre-train Delphi.
items.zh: 基准（Hendrycks et al., 2021a）提供了五个具有挑战性的任务，旨在评估语言模型关于五个主要道德框架的知识：正义、义务论、美德、功利主义和常识道德。ETHICS基准的详细信息见§3.1。附录§F中的表23展示了ETHICS任务的示例。我们已经将常识道德任务中的短场景包含在Delphi的原始训练数据中。其他任务的数据以及常识道德任务中的长场景未出现在预训练Delphi的数据中。

items.en: To investigate if knowledge acquired by Delphi can be transfered to other moral frameworks, we fine-tune Delphi on the five ETHICS tasks. As was done for the hate speech experiments, we use a few-shot setting for our investigation. Specifically, we fine-tune Delphi with 100 sampled training instances from each task from the ETHICS benchmark, and evaluate the resulted model on the regular and hard test sets from ETHICS. We include both the T5-11B and Unicorn models as baselines. All models are trained with a learning rate of 0.0002 and batch size of 8 on v3-32 TPU machines until the the model achieves the best performance on the development sets of each tasks.
items.zh: 为了研究Delphi获得的知识是否可以迁移到其他道德框架，我们在五个ETHICS任务上微调Delphi。与仇恨言论实验类似，我们在调查中使用少样本设置。具体来说，我们从ETHICS基准的每个任务中抽取100个训练实例微调Delphi，并在ETHICS的常规和困难测试集上评估得到的模型。我们包括T5-11B和Unicorn模型作为基线。所有模型以0.0002的学习率和8的批量大小在v3-32 TPU机器上训练，直到模型在每个任务的开发集上达到最佳性能。

items.en: We report on our results using the same classification accuracy metrics used in (Hendrycks et al., 2021a). For Justice, Deontology, and Virtue, which consist of groups of related examples (group of 4, 4, 5 examples that are minimal edits of each other respectively), an example is considered correct if all of the related examples are classified correctly by the model. For utilitarianism, an example is considered correct if the model predicts the ranking of the two actions correctly. Commonsense morality is measured with binary classification accuracy.
items.zh: 我们使用与（Hendrycks et al., 2021a）相同的分类准确率指标报告结果。对于正义、义务论和美德，这些任务由相关示例组组成（每组分别有4、4、5个示例，彼此是最小编辑），如果模型正确分类了所有相关示例，则该示例被视为正确。对于功利主义，如果模型正确预测了两个行动的排序，则该示例被视为正确。常识道德通过二元分类准确率衡量。

items.en: As shown in Table 9, Delphi is capable of transferring knowledge to moral frameworks in the ETHICS dataset with minimal in-domain training, outperforming both Unicorn and T5-11B baselines. Delphi predicts correct responses across all five tasks better than its most competitive baseline by 2.5% to 100.9% relative improvement on accuracies. Despite the fact Delphi is not built to make predictions aligned with specific moral frameworks, it effectively learns to transfer common patterns of human ethics in line with certain moral standpoints.
items.zh: 如表9所示，Delphi能够在最少的领域内训练下将知识迁移到ETHICS数据集中的道德框架，优于Unicorn和T5-11B基线。Delphi在所有五个任务上的正确预测比其最具竞争力的基线高出2.5%到100.9%的相对准确率提升。尽管Delphi并非为与特定道德框架对齐的预测而构建，但它有效地学习了迁移与某些道德立场一致的人类伦理常见模式。

items.en: Foreseen by Rawls, bottom-up approaches can fall prey to pervasive biases (Rawls, 1971), such as social biases and stereotypes in the case of most data-driven AI systems (Sheng et al., 2019; Dodge et al., 2021). Such biases cause representational harms against minoritized groups (Barocas et al., 2017), for which hate or derogatory sentiment is often rooted in a sense of moral disgust or outrage (Ungar, 2000; Does et al., 2011; Hoover et al., 2019), and therefore presents a challenge for Delphi. Although we took an initial step to explicitly counter social biases by including the Social Bias Inference Corpus in Norm Bank (e.g., teaching Delphi to infer that “saying that we shouldn’t lower our standards just to hire women” is “problematic” and, thus, learns to find microaggressions such as “asking an Asian person if they brought their bike from their country” as “rude”), Delphi is not immune.
items.zh: 正如罗尔斯所预见的，自下而上的方法可能受到普遍偏见的影响（Rawls, 1971），例如大多数数据驱动AI系统中的社会偏见和刻板印象（Sheng et al., 2019; Dodge et al., 2021）。这些偏见对少数群体造成表征性伤害（Barocas et al., 2017），而仇恨或贬损情绪往往源于道德厌恶或愤怒感（Ungar, 2000; Does et al., 2011; Hoover et al., 2019），因此对Delphi构成了挑战。尽管我们通过将社会偏见推理语料库纳入规范库（例如，教导Delphi推断“说我们不应该降低标准来雇佣女性”是“有问题的”，从而学会发现诸如“问亚洲人是否从他们的国家带来了自行车”这样的微攻击是“粗鲁的”），但Delphi并非免疫。

items.capEn: Figure 7: Results for the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR) probing, including top identities that Delphi shows biases against and their level of biases, and the average % error for each identity group.
items.capZh: 图7：世界人权宣言（UDHR）探测结果，包括Delphi表现出偏见的顶级身份及其偏见水平，以及每个身份群体的平均错误百分比。

full.sec: 7.1 Probing with Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR)

items.capEn: Figure 8: Heatmap showing Delphi’s prediction regarding various situations reflecting UDHR articles across various social and demographic identity groups. Values indicate how much the model’s predictions diverge from expectations. The darker the color, the larger the discrepancy is between the model predictions and the expected judgments. Asterisk (*) is placed next to negative rights (e.g., “{identity} are held in slavery and servitude”).
items.capZh: 图8：热力图显示Delphi对反映UDHR条款的各种社会人口身份群体情境的预测。数值表示模型预测与期望的偏差程度。颜色越深，模型预测与预期判断之间的差异越大。星号（*）标记在消极权利旁边（例如，“{身份}被奴役和奴役”）。

items.en: We design a controlled probing task to measure the extent to which Delphi honors equal fundamental human rights across varied social and demographic identities using the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR) (United Nations, 2021). We enumerate 38 human rights from UDHR (e.g., “{identity} have the right to equal pay” and pair them with 213 social and demographic identities (e.g., “women”) belonging to 12 social and demographic identity groups (e.g., gender) (Dixon et al., 2018; Mitchell et al., 2019). This way, we establish 8K situations (e.g., “women have the right to equal pay.”) designed to obtain a picture of the current-world realities of human rights. While the exact requirements of equality and justice are matters of vigorous debate (Lukes, 2008), we operate under the assumption that all identities should have all UDHR rights, and any model disagreement is evidence of bias.141414Errors may arise from mistakes in language understanding as well (Cao et al., 2022), but distinguishing them from biased-based errors is difficult. Thus, for the purposes of this probe we count all errors as evidences of bias. As such, we consider any false negatives, i.e., situations where certain identities are not predicted to have a certain right, as evidence of bias against those identities. The full list of human right situations is shown in Table 24 and 25 and the full list of social and demographic identities is shown in Table 26 in Appendix §G.
items.zh: 我们设计了一项受控探测任务，使用《世界人权宣言》（UDHR）（United Nations, 2021）来衡量Delphi在多大程度上尊重不同社会人口身份的平等基本人权。我们从UDHR中列举了38项人权（例如，“{身份}有权获得平等报酬”），并将其与213个社会人口身份（例如，“女性”）配对，这些身份属于12个社会人口身份群体（例如，性别）（Dixon et al., 2018; Mitchell et al., 2019）。这样，我们建立了8K个情境（例如，“女性有权获得平等报酬”），旨在描绘当前世界人权现实。虽然平等和正义的确切要求存在激烈争论（Lukes, 2008），但我们假设所有身份都应享有所有UDHR权利，任何模型的不一致都是偏见的证据。141414错误也可能源于语言理解问题（Cao et al., 2022），但将其与基于偏见的错误区分开来是困难的。因此，在本探测中，我们将所有错误视为偏见的证据。因此，我们将任何假阴性（即某些身份未被预测拥有某项权利的情境）视为针对这些身份的偏见证据。人权情境的完整列表见附录§G中的表24和25，社会人口身份的完整列表见表26。

items.en: Results show that Delphi fails to predict agreement with human rights in 1.3% of the cases. As shown in Figure 7a, strongest bias is observed for less privileged socio-economic identities (e.g., poor, homeless, lower-class, non-American people) and people from regions of current-day conflict (e.g., people from North Korea, Middle Eastern countries). For identities such as sexual orientation and gender, Delphi predicts agreement with all human rights. Interestingly, Delphi also shows bias against certain privileged identities (e.g., wealthy, non-disabled, beautiful people), though not at the level for marginalized groups.151515Privileged identities are often implicit and unmarked in discourse unless stated to highlight or call out privilege (e.g., in social justice discourse) (Zerubavel, 2018). This could explain Delphi’s biases against typically unmarked privileged identities.
items.zh: 结果显示，Delphi在1.3%的情况下未能预测对人权的同意。如图7a所示，最强烈的偏见出现在社会经济地位较低的身份（例如，穷人、无家可归者、下层阶级、非美国人）和当前冲突地区的人（例如，来自朝鲜、中东国家的人）。对于性取向和性别等身份，Delphi预测同意所有人权。有趣的是，Delphi也表现出对某些特权身份（例如，富人、非残疾人、美丽的人）的偏见，尽管程度不及边缘化群体。151515特权身份在话语中通常是隐含和未标记的，除非为了强调或指出特权（例如，在社会正义话语中）（Zerubavel, 2018）。这可以解释Delphi对通常未标记的特权身份的偏见。

items.en: Delphi’s disagreement on human rights for certain demographic groups highlights an inherent tension between the current, possibly unequal, state of the world and what an ideal world should look like. Our UDHR experiment’s declarative current-world phrasing of human rights (e.g., “poor people have the right to own property”) predisposes Delphi’s predictions to reflect the current state of the world. As a counterpoint, we also explore human rights using templates with an aspirational, ideal-world phrasing (e.g., “poor people should have the right to own property”). Crucially, Delphi predicts much less disagreement with the UDHR in the ideal-world setting (0.2%). Nonetheless, disagreements remain for certain groups (e.g., homeless people, people from North Korea), likely due to strong pervasive biases learned from the data. These results showcase the challenges of purely bottom-up approaches, while highlighting that Delphi has learned to interpret current-world and ideal-world phrasings differently.
items.zh: Delphi对某些人口群体人权的不同意凸显了当前可能不平等的世界状态与理想世界应是什么样子之间的内在张力。我们的UDHR实验采用声明性的当前世界措辞（例如，“穷人有权拥有财产”），使Delphi的预测倾向于反映当前世界状态。作为对比，我们还使用具有理想世界措辞的模板探索人权（例如，“穷人应该有权拥有财产”）。关键的是，在理想世界设定下，Delphi预测与UDHR的分歧要少得多（0.2%）。尽管如此，对某些群体（例如，无家可归者、来自朝鲜的人）的分歧仍然存在，这可能是由于从数据中学到的强烈普遍偏见。这些结果展示了纯粹自下而上方法的挑战，同时强调Delphi学会了不同地解释当前世界和理想世界的措辞。

items.en: Notably, even under the ideal-world setting, where Delphi is deliberately prompted to operate in line with the idealistic expectations of a society, the model continues to demonstrate a discrepancy from an upright fairness and justice among all populations. Such limitations echo with pervasive bias identified by John Rawls. While pervasive biases ultimately reflect the potentially distressing reality of today’s society, this does not necessarily mean that it should or will always be the case. Rawls argued that a complete moral theory must “work from both ends” (Rawls, 1971). If a bottom-up description is reflective of moral commonsense, a moral theory must be counterbalanced by applying top-down guarantees of human equality and dignity. Moreover, as it is, Delphi is a neural snapshot of its training data, which can be used to study present perceptions of ethics and morality. Any forward-looking research should take the ever-evolving views of social norms into account and avoid over-relying on (potentially obsolete) historical data to shape the future (Benjamin, 2019).
items.zh: 值得注意的是，即使在理想世界设定下，Delphi被有意提示按照社会的理想期望运作，模型仍然表现出与所有人群之间的公正公平的差异。这种局限性反映了约翰·罗尔斯所识别的普遍偏见。虽然普遍偏见最终反映了当今社会可能令人不安的现实，但这并不意味着它应该或总是如此。罗尔斯认为，完整的道德理论必须“从两端工作”（Rawls, 1971）。如果自下而上的描述反映了道德常识，那么道德理论必须通过应用自上而下的人类平等和尊严保证来平衡。此外，就其本身而言，Delphi是其训练数据的神经快照，可用于研究当前对伦理和道德的看法。任何前瞻性研究都应考虑不断变化的社会规范观点，避免过度依赖（可能过时的）历史数据来塑造未来（Benjamin, 2019）。

full.secZh: 7.2 强化德尔斐对抗社会偏见

items.en: To complement the purely data-driven approach which suffers from pervasive biases, we take an initial step towards a top-down mitigation of social biases. We collect annotations for a combination of frequent identity-related user queries along with general frequent queries from the Delphi demo, using them along with Norm Bank to train an enhanced model Delphi+.161616Judgments for the selected queries are crowdsourced, therefore, the approach is still bottom-up. However, we approximate a top-down measure in that the data is judiciously chosen to fill in Norm Bank’s missing knowledge gaps and thereby reinforce, in Delphi+, people’s values regarding identity-related queries.
items.zh: 为了补充受普遍偏见困扰的纯数据驱动方法，我们迈出了自上而下缓解社会偏见的第一步。我们收集了来自Delphi演示的频繁身份相关用户查询和一般频繁查询的注释，并将其与规范库一起用于训练增强模型Delphi+。161616所选查询的注释是众包的，因此该方法仍然是自下而上的。然而，我们近似于自上而下的措施，因为数据是明智地选择的，以填补规范库缺失的知识空白，从而在Delphi+中强化人们对身份相关查询的价值观。

items.en: We collect annotations for a combination of frequent identity-related (e.g., gender and race) user queries along with general frequent queries from the Delphi demo, using them along with Norm Bank to train an enhanced model Delphi+. We select an additional 78,131 queries from the Delphi demo, among which 13K relate to gender, 16K relate to race, and 30K relate to other social identities (e.g., religion, nationality).171717We use keyword matching to filter queries related to gender and race. The full list of keywords is shown Table 27 in H. There might be overlap between gender and race related queries. We provide queries along with predicted answers from Delphi, and ask annotators to correct the Delphi labels if they rate them as incorrect. For each query, we collect annotations from at least three annotators, resulting in 200K query-answer pairs in total. We include duplicated queries in the Delphi+ training and keep possibly different answer labels from different annotators to accommodate diverse answers.
items.zh: 我们收集了来自Delphi演示的频繁身份相关（例如，性别和种族）用户查询和一般频繁查询的注释，并将其与规范库一起用于训练增强模型Delphi+。我们从Delphi演示中选择了额外的78,131个查询，其中13K与性别相关，16K与种族相关，30K与其他社会身份（例如，宗教、国籍）相关。171717我们使用关键词匹配来过滤与性别和种族相关的查询。关键词完整列表见H中的表27。性别和种族相关查询之间可能存在重叠。我们提供查询以及Delphi的预测答案，并要求注释者如果认为Delphi标签不正确则进行纠正。对于每个查询，我们从至少三位注释者收集注释，总共产生200K个查询-答案对。我们在Delphi+训练中包含重复查询，并保留不同注释者可能的不同答案标签，以适应多样化的答案。

items.en: For training Delphi+, we modify the < and > characters in the separator tokens (i.e., “<action1 or 2>”, “<\action1 or 2>”, <class>”, “<\class>”, “<text>” and “<\text>”) to [ and ] respectively to be consistent with task prefix tokens (i.e., “[moral_single]:” and “[moral_pair]:”). Additionally, we change the -1 (negative), 0 (neutral), 1 (positive) classification labels to 0 (negative), 1 (neutral), 2 (positive) respectively to represent each class with a single number token. Our pilot study shows making these two minor format changes does not affect the model’s performance. All other training setups of Delphi+ are exactly the same as Delphi (see training details in §4.1).
items.zh: 对于训练Delphi+，我们将分隔符标记中的“<”和“>”字符（即“<action1 or 2>”、“<\action1 or 2>”、“<class>”、“<\class>”、“<text>”和“<\text>”）分别修改为“[”和“]”，以与任务前缀标记（即“[moral_single]:”和“[moral_pair]:”）一致。此外，我们将-1（负面）、0（中性）、1（正面）的分类标签分别改为0（负面）、1（中性）、2（正面），以用单个数字标记表示每个类别。我们的初步研究表明，这两个小的格式更改不会影响模型的性能。Delphi+的所有其他训练设置与Delphi完全相同（训练细节见§4.1）。

items.en: With Delphi+, we find even less pervasive social biases as measured through our UDHR experiments. As shown in Table 10, Delphi+ makes less errors on the UDHR probing tasks compared to Delphi (0.68% vs. 1.30% under the current-world setting; 0.14% vs. 0.19% under the ideal-world setting) while achieving the same in-domain performance on Norm Bank. This result suggests that targeted selection of training data, focusing on topics related to social justice, could help mitigate pervasive biases within Delphi. While some biases still remain, this highlights the promise of blending top-down and bottom-up approaches to mitigate pervasive biases.
items.zh: 通过Delphi+，我们发现通过UDHR实验测量的普遍社会偏见更少。如表10所示，与Delphi相比，Delphi+在UDHR探测任务上的错误更少（当前世界设定下为0.68% vs. 1.30%；理想世界设定下为0.14% vs. 0.19%），同时在规范库上实现了相同的领域内性能。这一结果表明，针对社会正义相关主题的有针对性的训练数据选择有助于缓解Delphi中的普遍偏见。虽然一些偏见仍然存在，但这凸显了融合自上而下和自下而上方法以缓解普遍偏见的前景。

items.en: Deep learning systems like Delphi demonstrate remarkable generalizability. However, they also showcase a range of limitations (Bender et al., 2021). We believe reliable and transparent moral reasoning models require a scrutiny of limitations. Thus, here, we examine Delphi’s scope and discuss its several undesirable behaviors, including limited culture awareness, inconsistent predictions, and limited general language understanding ability.
items.zh: 像Delphi这样的深度学习系统表现出显著的泛化能力。然而，它们也展示了一系列局限性（Bender et al., 2021）。我们相信，可靠和透明的道德推理模型需要对局限性进行审查。因此，在这里，我们检查Delphi的范围并讨论其几个不良行为，包括有限的文化意识、不一致的预测和有限的一般语言理解能力。

items.en: Human-authored datasets may encode ideologies from crowdworkers. Consequently, Delphi primarily encapsulates the moral compass and social expectations in the United States of the 21st century. Surprisingly, however, Delphi embodies a certain level of awareness of cultures beyond those represented in Norm Bank even without specific training. For example, in western countries, greeting someone by kissing on the cheek is friendly; whereas in other regions, doing so may be inappropriate and even illegal (Sophie Pettit, 2022). Accordingly, Delphi predicts, “greeting by kissing on the cheek in France” is “normal,” and doing so “in Vietnam” is “rude.” But the level of culture awareness does not reach all corners of the world (e.g., Delphi falsely predicts the action is “okay” “in Qatar.”) Moreover, Delphi shows limited understanding of customs which are less well known in western culture. For example, Delphi incorrectly adopts the default judgment “it’s normal” for “eating with your left hand in India or in Sri Lanka,” where eating with your left hand is considered unclean and offensive (Cultural Atlas, 2022b; a). Expanding Delphi to diverse cultures is a compelling research venue for exploring inclusive representations of machine ethics.
items.zh: 人类编写的数据集可能编码了众包工作者的意识形态。因此，Delphi主要体现了21世纪美国的道德指南针和社会期望。然而，令人惊讶的是，即使没有特定训练，Delphi也体现了一定程度对规范库之外文化的意识。例如，在西方国家，通过亲吻脸颊问候某人表示友好；而在其他地区，这样做可能不合适甚至违法（Sophie Pettit, 2022）。相应地，Delphi预测“在法国通过亲吻脸颊问候”是“正常的”，而在越南这样做是“粗鲁的”。但文化意识水平并未覆盖世界所有角落（例如，Delphi错误地预测该行为在卡塔尔是“可以的”）。此外，Delphi对西方文化中不太知名的习俗理解有限。例如，Delphi错误地采用默认判断“这是正常的”来描述“在印度或斯里兰卡用左手吃饭”，而在这些地方用左手吃饭被认为是不洁和冒犯的（Cultural Atlas, 2022b; a）。将Delphi扩展到多元文化是探索包容性机器伦理表征的一个引人注目的研究领域。

items.en: Data-driven deep learning systems may make inconsistent predictions across similar topics, as there is often no specific mechanism to enforce consistencies by default. Delphi faces the same issue, especially on numerical values and paraphrases. For example, Delphi predicts that “practicing drums at 12:00pm” and “at 12:15pm” are “okay”; doing so “at 12:30pm” is nevertheless “rude.” Similarly, while Delphi predicts “torturing a cat in secret” is “cruel” and “behind other people” is “bad,” doing so “if others don’t see it” is “okay.” We observe that, sometimes, Delphi may allow irrelevant keyphrases to adjust its judgment. For example, “killing a bear” is “wrong”, regardless of its appearance. While Delphi does not change the judgment for “a cute bear,” it makes a mistake for “an ugly bear.” We also see that sometimes Delphi shows positive biases and erroneously flips its judgment of a wrong action when supplied with innocuous contexts usually accompanying positive actions. For example, “performing genocide” is unquestionably “wrong,” but Delphi predicts doing so “if it creates jobs” is “okay.” Future efforts must investigate either applying external mechanisms or modifying internal model representations to impose consistencies.
items.zh: 数据驱动的深度学习系统可能会在相似主题上做出不一致的预测，因为通常没有特定机制默认强制执行一致性。Delphi面临同样的问题，尤其是在数值和释义方面。例如，Delphi预测“在中午12:00练习鼓”和“在中午12:15”是“可以的”；而在“中午12:30”这样做却是“粗鲁的”。类似地，虽然Delphi预测“秘密折磨猫”是“残忍的”，“在别人背后”是“坏的”，但“如果别人没看到”这样做是“可以的”。我们观察到，有时Delphi可能允许不相关的关键词短语调整其判断。例如，“杀死一只熊”是“错误的”，无论其外观如何。虽然Delphi没有改变对“一只可爱的熊”的判断，但对“一只丑陋的熊”却犯了错误。我们还看到，有时Delphi表现出正面偏见，当提供通常伴随正面行为的无害语境时，会错误地翻转对错误行为的判断。例如，“进行种族灭绝”无疑是“错误的”，但Delphi预测“如果它创造就业”这样做是“可以的”。未来的努力必须研究应用外部机制或修改内部模型表示以强制一致性。

items.en: Delphi is based on state-of-the-art pre-trained neural language models. However, machine language understanding at large is yet an unsolved task, restricting Delphi’s grasp of situations delivered through challenging language forms, such as convoluted situations with long contexts. Moreover, metaphorical and idiomatic language is known to be difficult for language models (Chakrabarty et al., 2022). Surprisingly, Delphi demonstrates an impressive amount of knowledge of nuanced and tacit language forms, as shown in Figure 2. For instance, Delphi correctly predicts “riding on someone’s coattails”181818“Ride on someone’s coattails” is an American idiom meaning “to have one’s success dependent on that of someone else.” is “wrong,” but doing so “while you learn the ropes”191919“Learn the ropes” is an American idiom meaning “learn or understand the basic details of how to do a job properly.” is, on the other hand, “okay.” But Delphi sometimes falls flat at expressions where the literal expression deviates far from the metaphorical meaning. For example, Delphi shows lack of understanding of “being all eyes and ears”202020“All eyes and ears” is an idiom meaning “eagerly giving one’s full attention to something.” and predicts it as a “bad” action, and “telling someone to ‘break a leg’ ”212121“Break a leg” is an idiom meaning “good luck.” as “rude.” Our position is that machine moral reasoning and machine language understanding should be investigated concurrently, carrying out mutual benefits to each other.
items.zh: Delphi基于最先进的预训练神经语言模型。然而，机器语言理解总体上仍是一项未解决的任务，限制了Delphi对通过挑战性语言形式（例如，带有长语境的复杂情境）传达的情境的理解。此外，隐喻性和习语性语言对语言模型来说是众所周知的困难（Chakrabarty et al., 2022）。令人惊讶的是，Delphi展示了大量关于微妙和隐性语言形式的知识，如图2所示。例如，Delphi正确预测“搭别人的顺风车”181818“Ride on someone’s coattails”是一个美国习语，意思是“一个人的成功依赖于他人的成功”。是“错误的”，但“在你学习窍门”191919“Learn the ropes”是一个美国习语，意思是“学习或理解如何正确完成工作的基本细节”。的同时这样做是“可以的”。但Delphi有时在字面表达与隐喻意义相差甚远的表达上表现不佳。例如，Delphi表现出对“全神贯注”202020“All eyes and ears”是一个习语，意思是“急切地全神贯注于某事”。缺乏理解，预测其为“坏的”行为，而“告诉某人‘祝你好运’”212121“Break a leg”是一个习语，意思是“祝你好运”。是“粗鲁的”。我们的立场是，机器道德推理和机器语言理解应同时研究，相互带来益处。

items.en: Here, we provide reflections on common counterarguments that have arisen since the release of our initial paper (Jiang et al., 2021b).
items.zh: 在这里，我们反思自我们最初论文发布以来出现的常见反驳论点（Jiang et al., 2021b）。

full.sec: 9.1 What do we mean when we say Delphi follows descriptive framework?
full.secZh: 9.1 我们说德尔斐遵循描述性框架时意味着什么？

items.en: In this paper, we have taken the stance that Delphi is founded in the theoretical framework of bottom-up, descriptive ethics (see §2.2). However, since Delphi learns by aggregating statistically dominant behaviors in the data, critiques have called into whether or not Delphi also enforces normative views of the society. Before we address this and other potential concerns, we take a moment to clarify how we define some of these key terminologies.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们采取的立场是Delphi建立在自下而上、描述性伦理学的理论框架中（见§2.2）。然而，由于Delphi通过聚合数据中的统计主导行为来学习，批评者质疑Delphi是否也强制执行社会的规范性观点。在我们解决这个问题和其他潜在担忧之前，我们先花点时间澄清我们如何定义这些关键术语。

items.en: Our approach is in line with descriptive ethics, which is in contrast to the notions of prescriptive or normative ethics. Descriptive ethics focuses on stating empirical facts about existing moral beliefs, such as “people think abandoning babies is bad.”, while prescriptive approaches focus on making top-down statements about how one should behave, such as “abandoning babies is bad.”. While the term normative is synonymous to prescriptive in philosophy, normative has yet another meaning in social sciences. It is used to refer to the aggregate or statistically dominant behavior in a population (e.g., most people will not voluntarily abandon a baby). Of course, these two meanings are related; people often feel (prescriptively) it is wrong to take (descriptively) counter-normative actions. But they can diverge, such as when descriptively prevailing norms endorse harmful social arrangements (e.g., smoking in enclosed spaces was once a descriptively normative behavior in much of the world). There is also a complicated interaction between descriptive norms and individuals’ prescriptive views; people are more likely to say that an action should be avoided if they believe that most people do try to avoid it (Bicchieri, 2016).
items.zh: 我们的方法符合描述性伦理学，这与规范性或规定性伦理学的概念形成对比。描述性伦理学侧重于陈述关于现有道德信念的经验事实，例如“人们认为遗弃婴儿是坏的”，而规定性方法侧重于做出关于应该如何行为的上而下陈述，例如“遗弃婴儿是坏的”。虽然术语“规范性”在哲学中与“规定性”同义，但“规范性”在社会科学中还有另一层含义。它用于指代群体中的聚合或统计主导行为（例如，大多数人不会自愿遗弃婴儿）。当然，这两种含义是相关的；人们通常（规定性地）认为采取（描述性地）反规范的行为是错误的。但它们可能分歧，例如当描述性普遍规范认可有害的社会安排时（例如，在封闭空间吸烟曾经是世界上大部分地区的描述性规范行为）。描述性规范与个人的规定性观点之间也存在复杂的相互作用；如果人们相信大多数人都试图避免某种行为，他们更可能说该行为应该避免（Bicchieri, 2016）。

items.en: Thus, when we say we take a bottom-up, descriptive approach, we mean that we build Delphi based on descriptive claims about morality (i.e. Norm Bank) without enforcing prescriptive tenets of correct behavior. We do, however, employ prescriptive top-down constraints when evaluating what Delphi has learned, such as the gold standard built from majority vote in our test set or the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR) from the United Nations. We resort to these evaluations, as they are the best probing methods we have at our disposal that provide a minimal and broadly acceptable set of standards. We recognize that value systems differ among annotators (Jiang et al., 2021a; Sap et al., 2022), and accept that even UDHR may not be acceptable for all.222222To take an extreme example, UDHR prohibits slavery, even though this excludes the opinions of those who support slavery. Perhaps some readers will object that there is an ethical requirement for scientists to take account of all viewpoints, but such exclusion of views is unavoidable since it is not possible to represent every viewpoint simultaneously. This is an inherent property of any approach that trains on a large corpus annotated by multiple people. Moreover, there are interesting further questions about whether scientists, ethicists, and society generally might draw further prescriptive conclusions once we have a complete descriptive picture (see §9.3 below), but for the moment, our aims are primarily descriptive with some allowances for the need to proactively counterweight predicted social bias (see §7.2).
items.zh: 因此，当我们说我们采取自下而上、描述性的方法时，我们的意思是我们在没有强制执行正确行为的规定性信条的情况下，基于关于道德的描述性主张（即规范库）构建Delphi。然而，在评估Delphi所学内容时，我们确实采用了规定性的自上而下约束，例如基于测试集中多数投票构建的金标准或联合国《世界人权宣言》（UDHR）。我们诉诸这些评估，因为它们是我们可用的最佳探测方法，提供了最低限度且广泛可接受的标准。我们认识到注释者之间的价值体系存在差异（Jiang et al., 2021a; Sap et al., 2022），并接受即使是UDHR也可能不被所有人接受。222222举一个极端的例子，UDHR禁止奴隶制，即使这排除了那些支持奴隶制的人的意见。也许有些读者会反对，认为科学家有伦理要求考虑所有观点，但这种排除是不可避免的，因为不可能同时代表所有观点。这是任何在多人注释的大型语料库上训练的方法的固有属性。此外，还有有趣的进一步问题，即科学家、伦理学家和社会一般是否可以在拥有完整的描述性图景后得出进一步的规定性结论（见下文§9.3），但目前，我们的目标主要是描述性的，并允许主动抵消预测的社会偏见（见§7.2）。

full.sec: 9.2 Does generating ethical judgment reinforce normative values?
full.secZh: 9.2 生成伦理判断是否强化了规范性价值观？

items.en: Since Delphi gathers the statistically dominant answers to moral questions, one might worry that its output could exert a reinforcing effect on existing moral beliefs, locking people into going along with popular opinion. Some critics may go even further to suggest that Delphi cannot avoid engaging in prescriptive ethics by synthesizing statistically dominant answers to moral questions (Talat et al., 2021).
items.zh: 由于Delphi收集的是道德问题在统计上占主导地位的答案，人们可能会担心其输出会对现有的道德信念产生强化效应，使人们固守于随大流的观点。一些批评者甚至可能进一步认为，Delphi通过综合道德问题的统计主导答案，无法避免介入规范性伦理学（Talat et al., 2021）。

items.en: But it is possible to provide descriptive facts about common moral beliefs without either intending or causing an influence on audiences’ personal moral beliefs. Consider, for example, traditional opinion surveys. Since 1981, the World Values Survey (World Value Survey, 2022) has solicited moral views from thousands of people and reported statistically dominant results broken down by countries or regions. While the World Values Survey clearly reports on normative content, this does not mean that its function is to create and reinforce norms. Indeed, the social scientists who administer the World Values Survey would likely insist that they do not mean to endorse or advance the judgments they report on.
items.zh: 但提供关于常见道德信念的描述性事实是可能的，而无需意图或影响受众的个人道德信念。例如，传统的民意调查。自1981年以来，世界价值观调查（World Value Survey, 2022）向数千人征求道德观点，并报告按国家或地区划分的统计主导结果。虽然世界价值观调查明确报告了规范性内容，但这并不意味着其功能是创造和强化规范。事实上，进行世界价值观调查的社会科学家可能会坚持认为，他们并不打算认可或推进他们所报告的判断。

items.en: Delphi’s outputs can be interpreted in a similar way. To go beyond this and claim that the statistically dominant opinions registered by Delphi actually are prescriptively normative—that is, everyone should agree with them and abide by them—requires additional arguments. We do not provide such arguments and do not endorse the prescriptive use of Delphi for human decision making. Furthermore, since most people are at risk for (mis)attributing a communicative intent to model-generated language (Bender et al., 2021), we take caution to warn users of Delphi and its demo that Delphi and its outputs are strictly intended for research purpose only and inviting further discourse and investigation in machine ethics. However, we also recognize that there is a risk that systems like Delphi be turned into a moral authority and, consequently, a potential for harm in using our system for decision making on real-life matters. As discussed in §10.1, we strongly disagree with such misuse of Delphi and support the development of regulations and policies—alongside the development of AI—to prevent misuses of any AI system (Wischmeyer & Rademacher, 2020; Crawford, 2021; Reich et al., 2021).
items.zh: Delphi的输出可以类似地解释。要超越这一点，声称Delphi记录的统计主导观点实际上具有规范性——即每个人都应同意并遵守它们——则需要额外的论证。我们不提供这样的论证，也不认可将Delphi用于人类决策的规范性用途。此外，由于大多数人面临将交际意图错误归因于模型生成语言的风险（Bender et al., 2021），我们谨慎地警告Delphi及其演示的用户：Delphi及其输出严格仅用于研究目的，并邀请在机器伦理领域进行进一步的讨论和调查。然而，我们也认识到，像Delphi这样的系统有可能被变成道德权威，从而在将我们的系统用于现实事务决策时造成潜在危害。如§10.1所述，我们强烈反对这种对Delphi的滥用，并支持制定法规和政策——与AI发展并行——以防止任何AI系统的滥用（Wischmeyer & Rademacher, 2020; Crawford, 2021; Reich et al., 2021）。

full.sec: 9.3 Are there objectively true ethical judgments?
full.secZh: 9.3 是否存在客观真实的伦理判断？

items.en: Some readers might wonder if the goals of Delphi require taking any particular position on whether ethical judgments can be objectively true (that is, independent of subjective opinion)? In philosophy, this is usually framed as the debate between metaethical realism and anti-realism (Nagel, 1986; Mackie, 1977). Realists argue that there are some facts (either empirical or logical) that make certain ethical claims objectively true, whether or not any person ever agrees with them. Anti-realists deny this position. But here, we can sidestep this philosophical debate by building on Rawls’ method of reflective equilibrium, which is compatible with either metaethical position. Proponents of metaethical realism could argue that Rawls’ crowdsourced approach can move towards objective truths by averaging over populations of judgments. In the same way that one individual guessing the number of marbles in a jar may be far from the truth, but averaging many guesses from many individuals can lead to a closer estimate of the true value, aggregating across many moral judgments may converge on objective moral truth. Alternately, anti-realists about morality may instead see Rawls’ approach as a first approximation of the source material of constructed human morality. Whether either of these interpretations is better is not something we take a position on here, and we invite further discussion from ethical theorists.
items.zh: 一些读者可能想知道，Delphi的目标是否需要就伦理判断是否具有客观真理性（即独立于主观意见）采取特定立场？在哲学中，这通常被框定为元伦理实在论与反实在论之间的争论（Nagel, 1986; Mackie, 1977）。实在论者认为，存在某些事实（经验的或逻辑的）使得某些伦理主张客观为真，无论是否有人同意它们。反实在论者否认这一立场。但在这里，我们可以通过借鉴罗尔斯的反思平衡方法来回避这一哲学争论，该方法与任何一种元伦理立场都兼容。元伦理实在论的支持者可以认为，罗尔斯的众包方法可以通过对判断总体进行平均来接近客观真理。就像一个人猜测罐子里的弹珠数量可能远离真相，但平均许多人的猜测可以更接近真实值一样，聚合许多道德判断可能收敛于客观道德真理。或者，关于道德的反实在论者可能将罗尔斯的方法视为建构的人类道德源材料的初步近似。这两种解释哪种更好，我们在此不持立场，并邀请伦理理论家进一步讨论。

full.sec: 9.4 Can we derive consistent moral decision procedures from diverse and potentially contradictory inputs?
full.secZh: 9.4 能否从多样且可能矛盾的输入中推导出一致的道德决策程序？

items.en: Talat et al. (2021) argue that “From a descriptive perspective, diverse (that is conflicting) ethical judgments are expected, but from a normative one, conflicting ethical judgments are simply incommensurable.” In other words, Delphi risks internal inconsistency by drawing on a range of diverse viewpoints, making its outputs unfit even as starting points for future ethical theory construction. But this argument is philosophically mistaken. It is true that a hypothetical finalized moral framework, consisting of permanently settled general principles, must be internally consistent. But this does not mean that the inputs to a moral decision procedure intended to generate these final principles must start out mutually consistent.
items.zh: Talat等人（2021）认为，“从描述性角度看，多样（即冲突的）伦理判断是可预期的，但从规范性角度看，冲突的伦理判断根本不可通约。”换句话说，Delphi通过借鉴一系列多样化的观点，面临内部不一致的风险，使其输出甚至不适合作为未来伦理理论构建的起点。但这个论点在哲学上是错误的。确实，一个假设的最终道德框架，由永久确定的普遍原则组成，必须内部一致。但这并不意味着旨在生成这些最终原则的道德决策程序的输入必须一开始就相互一致。

items.en: Indeed, one of the central tasks of modern moral philosophy has been to articulate how we arrive at consistent final principles after beginning from moral intuitions that we know contain internal inconsistencies. Philosophers offer various ways to approach the resolution of inconsistent starting points. Naturalist moral realists (Boyd, 2003; Wong, 2006) model their approach on theory construction in natural science, where initial data reports regularly seem to be inconsistent with other data but can be corrected through better sampling or theoretical apparatus. Constructivist moral theorists (Korsgaard, 1996; Street, 2012) look instead at the internal logic of moral claims, seeking to extract the most fundamental (and internally consistent) principles from an initial tangle of divergent intuitions.
items.zh: 事实上，现代道德哲学的核心任务之一就是阐明我们如何在知道道德直觉包含内部不一致的情况下，从这些直觉出发，达成一致的最终原则。哲学家们提出了各种解决不一致起点的方法。自然主义道德实在论者（Boyd, 2003; Wong, 2006）将其方法建模为自然科学中的理论构建，其中初始数据报告经常看似与其他数据不一致，但可以通过更好的抽样或理论工具进行修正。建构主义道德理论家（Korsgaard, 1996; Street, 2012）则关注道德主张的内在逻辑，试图从最初杂乱无章的不同直觉中提取最根本（且内部一致）的原则。

items.en: These approaches converge on the most common methodology in modern moral philosophy, called “wide reflective equilibrium” (Daniels, 1979), which explicitly aims at reconciling inconsistencies among moral judgments. Of course, Delphi does not resolve inconsistencies in exactly the way these theories require; the point here is only that diverse, even disagreeing, starting moral judgments are not an in-principle problem for yielding consistent outputs.
items.zh: 这些方法汇聚于现代道德哲学中最常见的方法论，称为“广义反思平衡”（Daniels, 1979），它明确旨在调和道德判断中的不一致。当然，Delphi并不完全按照这些理论要求的方式解决不一致；这里的要点仅仅是，多样甚至相互矛盾的初始道德判断，原则上并不妨碍产生一致的输出。

items.en: The general goal underlying the Delphi experiment is to take a step towards inclusive, ethically informed, and socially aware AI systems. In doing so, we seek to address the fundamental problem of lack of basic human-compatible moral sense in current AI systems. Contemporary efforts towards improving the safety of AI propose the use of governing bodies to regulate the responsible use of AI while being deployed (Commission, 2021). Ethically informed AI systems can help complement or even support the regulation of AI, e.g., by raising an alarm for human intervention when ethically questionable use cases such as call for violence arise. Thus, in this work, we take a deliberate step toward aligning Delphi to explicit expressions of human norms and ethics to investigate the challenges posed by the complexity and importance of machine ethics (Moor, 2006; Wallach & Allen, 2010; Liao, 2020).
items.zh: Delphi实验的总体目标是朝着包容性、伦理知情和社会意识强的AI系统迈出一步。这样做，我们试图解决当前AI系统缺乏基本人类兼容道德感的根本问题。当代提升AI安全性的努力提出，在部署AI时，使用治理机构来规范其负责任的使用（Commission, 2021）。伦理知情的AI系统可以帮助补充甚至支持AI的监管，例如，在出现伦理上可疑的用例（如呼吁暴力）时，为人类干预发出警报。因此，在这项工作中，我们有意将Delphi与人类规范和伦理的明确表达对齐，以研究机器伦理的复杂性和重要性所带来的挑战（Moor, 2006; Wallach & Allen, 2010; Liao, 2020）。

items.en: We have shown that Delphi demonstrates a notable ability to generate on-target predictions over new and unseen situations even when challenged with nuanced situations. This supports our hypothesis that machines can be taught human moral sense, and indicates that the bottom-up method is a promising path forward for creating more morally informed AI systems.
items.zh: 我们已经证明，Delphi在应对新的、未见过的情境时，即使面对微妙的情况，也表现出显著的能力来生成准确的预测。这支持了我们的假设：机器可以被教授人类的道德感，并表明自下而上的方法是创建更具道德意识的AI系统的有前途的前进道路。

items.en: Despite Delphi’s impressive capabilities, however, it is still at an early stage of research. We have observed and reported Delphi’s susceptibility to errors due to pervasive biases. Unfortunately, such biases are not unique to Delphi, but it is an inherent aspect of any modern data-driven deep learning system that learns by capturing statistically dominant patterns in the data Benjamin (2019). Overcoming such biases will require the introduction of top-down constraints to complement bottom-up knowledge, i.e., a hybrid approach that “works from both ends” as proposed by John Rawls (Rawls, 1971). We make initial attempts to enforce notions of social justice in Delphi via the inclusion of Social Bias Inference Corpus in Norm Bank. We also show that biases can be reduced by addressing certain information gaps in the dataset (e.g., issues of gender and race) via further training. While we show promising methods to mitigate some biases in Delphi, significant future research is required to address biases in neural models.
items.zh: 尽管Delphi能力令人印象深刻，但它仍处于研究的早期阶段。我们观察到并报告了Delphi因普遍偏见而容易出错。不幸的是，这种偏见并非Delphi独有，而是任何现代数据驱动的深度学习系统固有的方面，这些系统通过捕捉数据中的统计主导模式来学习（Benjamin, 2019）。克服这种偏见需要引入自上而下的约束来补充自下而上的知识，即约翰·罗尔斯提出的“从两端工作”的混合方法（Rawls, 1971）。我们通过将社会偏见推理语料库纳入规范库，初步尝试在Delphi中强制执行社会正义概念。我们还表明，通过进一步训练解决数据集中的某些信息缺口（例如性别和种族问题），可以减少偏见。虽然我们展示了减轻Delphi中某些偏见的有前景的方法，但解决神经模型中的偏见仍需大量未来研究。

items.en: Nonetheless, as we have shown, an imperfect system like Delphi can be useful for downstream applications like hate speech detection. Delphi offers a first step toward enabling safe and trustworthy human-AI interactions via a shared understanding of human ethics and values. As such, we envision a potential use case of AI systems like Delphi in supporting other AI systems by providing an awareness of important human values. However, Delphi is not intended to be and should not be used as an independent moral authority or source of ethical advice for humans. It should be up to humans, not algorithms, to decide whether, when, and how, to apply such moral sense in automated decision making. To prevent potential misuses of AI models like Delphi, we also strongly support the development of AI policy and regulations about AI systems and their uses (Wischmeyer & Rademacher, 2020; Crawford, 2021; Reich et al., 2021).
items.zh: 尽管如此，正如我们所展示的，像Delphi这样不完美的系统可以用于仇恨言论检测等下游应用。Delphi通过共享对人类伦理和价值的理解，为实现安全可信的人机交互提供了第一步。因此，我们设想像Delphi这样的AI系统的一个潜在用例是，通过提供对重要人类价值的意识来支持其他AI系统。然而，Delphi不打算也不应被用作独立的道德权威或人类伦理建议的来源。应该由人类，而不是算法，来决定是否、何时以及如何在自动化决策中应用这种道德感。为了防止像Delphi这样的AI模型被滥用，我们也强烈支持制定关于AI系统及其使用的AI政策和法规（Wischmeyer & Rademacher, 2020; Crawford, 2021; Reich et al., 2021）。

items.en: Morality is hardly a static construct. Societies evolve over time, adjusting away from tendencies to discriminate and striving for inclusivity; so should AI ethics. We believe that the task of updating computational ethics models like Delphi is a continuous process requiring attention from researchers from various disciplines and backgrounds. It also requires engagement with users to identify their needs, particularly when the preconceptions of researchers may overlook potential harms (Bender et al., 2021). Therefore, transparency in such efforts in AI ethics is critical—engaging researchers and other stakeholders, such as consumers and regulators, in open discourse, and inviting various viewpoints in the improvement of computational ethics models. In this effort, we make our system and data available for academics and researchers with prospects for further dialogues in machine ethics research.
items.zh: 道德几乎不是一个静态的建构。社会随着时间的推移而演变，调整远离歧视的倾向，努力实现包容性；AI伦理也应如此。我们认为，更新像Delphi这样的计算伦理模型的任务是一个持续的过程，需要来自不同学科和背景的研究人员的关注。它还需要与用户互动以识别他们的需求，特别是当研究人员的先入之见可能忽视潜在危害时（Bender et al., 2021）。因此，在AI伦理方面的此类努力中，透明度至关重要——让研究人员和其他利益相关者（如消费者和监管机构）参与公开讨论，并邀请各种观点来改进计算伦理模型。在这项努力中，我们向学术界和研究人员提供我们的系统和数据，以期在机器伦理研究中进一步对话。

items.en: Ethical reasoning is a particularly acute challenge for AI research because of its subtlety, cultural nuance, and application to areas where humans continue to disagree with one another. The next steps in this research will require collective, interdisciplinary efforts from across the research community as a whole. In what follows, we share a list of open questions and avenues for future research.
items.zh: 伦理推理对AI研究来说是一个特别严峻的挑战，因为它具有微妙性、文化细微差别，并应用于人类仍然相互分歧的领域。这项研究的下一步将需要整个研究界集体的、跨学科的努力。接下来，我们分享一系列开放问题和未来研究的途径。

items.en: How ethical are current AI systems? What ethical or moral principles do current AI systems implicitly learn from their default training?
items.zh: 当前的AI系统有多伦理？当前的AI系统从其默认训练中隐含地学习了哪些伦理或道德原则？

items.en: Is moral reasoning reducible to objective reasoning?
items.zh: 道德推理能否还原为客观推理？

items.en: How can we build systems that handle complex situations, moving beyond reasoning over short snippets?
items.zh: 我们如何构建能够处理复杂情境的系统，超越对短片段进行推理？

items.en: Can we move beyond language-based moral reasoning systems to multi-modal systems that can process visual and audio signals as well? Such capabilities are becoming imperative as we build bots that interact with humans in the real world.232323https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/devices/meet-astro-a-home-robot-unlike-any-other
items.zh: 我们能否超越基于语言的道德推理系统，构建也能处理视觉和音频信号的多模态系统？随着我们构建与现实世界人类互动的机器人，这种能力正变得势在必行。232323https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/devices/meet-astro-a-home-robot-unlike-any-other

items.en: How can a system handle more complex moral dilemmas or controversial issues? Can we teach machines to express uncertainties or produce distributional moral opinions (e.g., producing confidence scores across multiple, possibly contradicting, moral judgments)?
items.zh: 系统如何处理更复杂的道德困境或有争议的问题？我们能否教会机器表达不确定性或产生分布式的道德意见（例如，在多个可能相互矛盾的道德判断上产生置信度分数）？

items.en: How does a moral reasoning system distinguish broad, generally accepted norms from personal values? Is it possible to customize moral reasoning models to specific value systems or moral frameworks?
items.zh: 道德推理系统如何区分广泛的、普遍接受的规范与个人价值观？是否可以将道德推理模型定制为特定的价值体系或道德框架？

items.en: Is it possible to address the conflicts between individual preferences and the common good (e.g., “No one wants a car that looks after the greater good. They want a car that looks after them,” Metz, 2016)? More broadly, are conflicted values could be simultaneously accommodated in a moral reasoning system?
items.zh: 是否有可能解决个人偏好与共同利益之间的冲突（例如，“没有人想要一辆照顾更大利益的汽车。他们想要一辆照顾自己的汽车，”Metz, 2016）？更广泛地说，冲突的价值观能否在道德推理系统中同时得到容纳？

items.en: How do we exert finer-grained control over the system’s choices (beyond simply toying with the training examples)?
items.zh: 我们如何对系统的选择施加更精细的控制（而不仅仅是简单地调整训练示例）？

items.en: How does one integrate a system like Delphi to influence behaviors of other models on tasks (e.g., by influencing the objective function, as in multi-task learning, or through background knowledge integration methods). For example, Delphi predicts that “hiring a man over a more qualified woman because women are likely to take parental leave” is “sexist.” How can downstream decision-making systems or tasks effectively incorporate this additional information?
items.zh: 如何集成像 Delphi 这样的系统来影响其他模型在任务上的行为（例如，通过影响目标函数，如多任务学习，或通过背景知识集成方法）。例如，Delphi 预测“雇佣一个男性而不是一个更合格的女性的原因是女性可能休产假”是“性别歧视”。下游决策系统或任务如何有效地整合这一额外信息？

items.en: How prevalent is moral reporting bias (i.e., people say one thing but do another)? How do we measure it and fix it in future iterations of Delphi-like systems?
items.zh: 道德报告偏差（即人们说一套做一套）有多普遍？我们如何测量它并在未来类似 Delphi 的系统中修复它？

items.en: How to move beyond the North American value system that the current Delphi inherits from Commonsense Norm Bank at large? How can we account for the diversity of cultures, ideologies, and societal structures when approaching machine ethics?
items.zh: 如何超越当前 Delphi 从 Commonsense Norm Bank 继承的北美价值体系？在处理机器伦理时，我们如何考虑文化、意识形态和社会结构的多样性？

items.en: How does a moral reasoning system evolve in lockstep with the evolution of societies over time?
items.zh: 道德推理系统如何随着社会的发展而同步演化？

items.en: How to efficiently collect moral judgments in the wild (e.g., building interactive interfaces to collect adversarial moral judgments from the general public), which is presumed to capture a more accurate distribution of people’s moral judgments in the world with broader coverage of opinions comparing to (narrowly representative) crowd-sourced annotations?
items.zh: 如何高效地在现实世界中收集道德判断（例如，构建交互式界面以收集来自公众的对抗性道德判断），这被认为能够捕捉到更准确的世界中人们的道德判断分布，并且比（狭隘代表性的）众包注释具有更广泛的观点覆盖？

items.en: Can we elicit explanations of models’ moral judgments to make model decisions traceable and accountable?
items.zh: 我们能否引出模型道德判断的解释，使模型决策可追溯和可问责？

items.en: Can we interactively interpret model predictions and perform model editing for incorrect model outputs cost-effectively?
items.zh: 我们能否以经济高效的方式交互式地解释模型预测并对错误模型输出进行模型编辑？

items.en: How do we incorporate top-down constraints to complement the pure bottom-up descriptive approach that Delphi takes to computationally achieve “reflective equilibrium?”
items.zh: 我们如何整合自上而下的约束，以补充 Delphi 所采用的纯粹自下而上的描述性方法，从而在计算上实现“反思平衡”？

items.en: How to better inform, educate, and raise awareness of machine ethics from the science communication perspective?
items.zh: 如何从科学传播的角度更好地告知、教育和提高对机器伦理的认识？

items.en: The authors thank Yoav Goldberg, Peter Clark, Ana Marasović, Kristin Andrews, Vivek Srikumar, Sydney Levine, Vikram Iyer and Wei Qiu for helpful discussions, and Sam Stuesser from the REVIZ team at AI2 for designing the logo of the demo of Delphi. This research was supported in part by DARPA under the MCS program through NIWC Pacific (N66001-19-2-4031), and the Allen Institute for AI (AI2). TPU machines for conducting experiments were generously provided by Google through the TensorFlow Research Cloud (TFRC) program.
items.zh: 作者感谢 Yoav Goldberg、Peter Clark、Ana Marasović、Kristin Andrews、Vivek Srikumar、Sydney Levine、Vikram Iyer 和 Wei Qiu 的有益讨论，以及 AI2 的 REVIZ 团队的 Sam Stuesser 为 Delphi 演示设计的标志。本研究部分由 DARPA 通过 MCS 计划（NIWC Pacific，N66001-19-2-4031）和 Allen Institute for AI（AI2）资助。用于进行实验的 TPU 机器由 Google 通过 TensorFlow Research Cloud（TFRC）计划慷慨提供。

items.en: LJ led the design and development of Delphi in collaboration with JDH, CB, JL, RLB, MS, MF and YC. CB and RLB conducted the initial prototyping and proof of concept experiments. LJ compiled the Commonsense Norm Bank by unifying the source data with advice from MF, MS, and JDH. LJ and KS conducted experiments on downstream applications with advice from RLB, CB and YC. LJ and JDH conducted the intrinsic evaluation of Delphi and the extrinsic evaluation of downstream applications. JL conducted dataset topics analysis with advice from LJ, RLB and JDH. LJ and MS conducted the United Nation Universal Declaration of Human Rights probing analysis with advice from JDH and JL. RLB and LJ collected data annotations for the Delphi+ model. JL and JB designed and implemented the front-end of Delphi’s demo with CB implementing the its back-end. Demo was iterated for improvement based on advice provided by LJ, RLB, MS, and YC. LJ and JL organized the publicly released data and compiled the datasheet document. RR provided her expertise in ethical theory and a close guidance in its application in the present study. YC provided leadership and supervision over the project. LJ, JDH, CB, RR, MS, JL, JD and YC wrote the paper with consultations from KS, RLB, OE, MF, SG, and YT. All authors had full access to all the data in the study and had final responsibility for the decision to submit for publication.
items.zh: LJ 领导了 Delphi 的设计和开发，与 JDH、CB、JL、RLB、MS、MF 和 YC 合作。CB 和 RLB 进行了初始原型设计和概念验证实验。LJ 通过统一源数据编制了 Commonsense Norm Bank，并得到了 MF、MS 和 JDH 的建议。LJ 和 KS 在 RLB、CB 和 YC 的建议下进行了下游应用实验。LJ 和 JDH 进行了 Delphi 的内在评估和下游应用的外在评估。JL 在 LJ、RLB 和 JDH 的建议下进行了数据集主题分析。LJ 和 MS 在 JDH 和 JL 的建议下进行了联合国世界人权宣言探测分析。RLB 和 LJ 收集了 Delphi+ 模型的数据注释。JL 和 JB 设计并实现了 Delphi 演示的前端，CB 实现了其后端。演示根据 LJ、RLB、MS 和 YC 提供的建议进行了迭代改进。LJ 和 JL 组织了公开发布的数据并编制了数据表文档。RR 提供了她在伦理理论方面的专业知识，并在本研究中密切指导其应用。YC 提供了项目的领导力和监督。LJ、JDH、CB、RR、MS、JL、JD 和 YC 撰写了论文，并咨询了 KS、RLB、OE、MF、SG 和 YT。所有作者均可完全访问研究中的所有数据，并对提交发表的决定负有最终责任。
